decomposed_criteria.criterion_text: Low back pain or radicular symptoms persist for at least 6 weeks
Symptoms have not meaningfully improved with conservative treatment
Imaging results are expected to affect management
Neurologic deficit or red flag symptoms documented

extracted_facts.fact_text: No neurologic deficits documented on exam
Patient prefers MRI over physical therapy
extracted_facts.source_quote: Patient prefers MRI over physical therapy

extracted_facts.fact_text: MRI requested due to persistent pain only
extracted_facts.source_quote: requests MRI because pain is persistent

criteria_evaluation.explanation: Documented duration is below the 6-week requirement.
No completed conservative treatment trial is documented.

criteria_evaluation.patient_evidence: No trial completion or response documented
criteria_evaluation.explanation: Cannot assess treatment failure without a documented trial.

criteria_evaluation.patient_evidence: No treatment plan tied to imaging
criteria_evaluation.explanation: Note does not explain how MRI would change management.
No neurologic deficit or red flag symptoms are documented.

evidence_gaps.description: Conservative therapy not documented as attempted or completed
No neurologic deficit or red flag documented
No clear statement that imaging would change management

rationale: Neither pathway is met. Duration and conservative therapy requirements are not satisfied, and there is no documented link between imaging and management planning.
suggested_next_steps: Request documentation of conservative treatment trial and response
Clarify expected impact of MRI on treatment plan
Re-evaluate if symptoms persist beyond 6 weeks with documented conservative care
policy_citations: Pathway 1: Persistent symptoms after conservative management
